docs.title: Steam Client
docs.text:
Steam Client is a Python library for interacting with a local Steam installation.

Use this site to quickly get started, see common usage patterns, and browse API docs generated from real source modules in this repository.

docs.title: What you can do
docs.text:
• Read installed library folders and games
  • Access game metadata and artwork paths
  • Read local users and non-Steam shortcuts
  • Launch Steam URI commands

docs.title: Project links
docs.text:
• Source: https://github.com/garulf/steam-client
  • PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/steam-client/

docs.title: Install
docs.text: pip install steam-client

docs.title: First script
docs.text:
from steam_client.steam import Steam

steam = Steam()
print(steam.library_folders)

On Windows, you can use registry discovery:

from steam_client.utils import steam_from_registry

steam = steam_from_registry()
print(steam.app_cache)

docs.title: List games
docs.text:
from steam_client.steam import Steam

steam = Steam()
for game in steam.library.games():
    print(game.appid, game.name)

docs.title: Find a game
docs.text:
game = steam.library.game_by_id("440")
if game:
    print(game.name)

game = steam.library.game_by_name("Portal 2")
if game:
    print(game.appid)

docs.title: Use Steam URI commands
docs.text:
from steam_client.commands import Commands, SteamWindows

commands = Commands()
commands.run_game_id("440")
commands.open(SteamWindows.FRIENDS)

docs.title: Read users and shortcuts
docs.text:
for user in steam.users:
    print(user.user.data.PersonaName)

for shortcut in steam.library.shortcuts():
    print(shortcut.appname)

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
The API is organized into logical groups. Each page is generated directly from the source modules in this repository.

Module Description Steam Main entry point — wraps a local Steam installation Library Read installed games and non-Steam shortcuts Games & Apps Game, Shortcut, and the shared App base class Commands Send Steam URI commands to the local client Users Read logged-in Steam users Utilities Helper functions (e.g. Windows registry discovery)

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
Commands builds and dispatches steam:// URI commands to the local Steam client via webbrowser.open. SteamWindows is a StrEnum of all openable Steam windows.

from steam_client.commands import Commands, SteamWindows

commands = Commands()
commands.run_game_id("440")
commands.open(SteamWindows.FRIENDS)

docs.title: SteamWindows
docs.text:
Bases: StrEnum

Enumeration of Steam client windows.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: A collection of commands for the Steam client.

docs.title: run_game_id
docs.text:
run_game_id(app_id: str) -> None

Launches game with the specified ID in the Steam client.

Source code in steam_client/commands.py 
def run_game_id(self, app_id: str) -> None:
    """Launches game with the specified ID in the Steam client."""
    self._open('rungameid', app_id)

docs.title: App
docs.text: Abstract base class shared by Game and Shortcut. Provides the common interface for name, appid, artwork paths, and launching.

docs.title: Game
docs.text:
Represents an installed Steam game read from an appmanifest_*.acf file.

game = steam.library.game_by_id("440")
print(game.name, game.header)

docs.title: Game
docs.text:
Game(library_cache_path: Path, library_path: str, appid: str)

Bases: App

Represents a Steam game.

Source code in steam_client/game.py 
def __init__(self, library_cache_path: Path, library_path: str, appid: str):
    self._library_cache_path = Path(library_cache_path)
    self.library_path = library_path
    self._appid = appid
    self._icon: Path | None = None
    self._name: str | None = None
    super().__init__()

docs.title: Shortcut
docs.text:
Represents a non-Steam game shortcut. App IDs are computed using the same CRC32 algorithm Steam uses internally.

for shortcut in steam.library.shortcuts():
    print(shortcut.appname, shortcut.grid)

docs.title: Shortcut
docs.text:
Shortcut(user: LoginUser, data: ShortcutEntry)

Bases: App

Represents a Non-Steam Game shortcut.

Source code in steam_client/shortcut.py 
def __init__(self, user: LoginUser, data: ShortcutEntry):
    self._data = data
    self._user = user
    super().__init__()

docs.title: Library
docs.text:
Library provides access to installed games and non-Steam shortcuts across all Steam library folders on the machine.

for game in steam.library.games():
    print(game.appid, game.name)

docs.title: Library
docs.text:
Library(steam: 'Steam')

Represents the Steam library.

Source code in steam_client/library.py 
def __init__(self, steam: 'Steam'):
    self._steam = steam
    self._libraries_hash: Optional[str] = None
    self._libraries: List[LibraryFolder] = []

docs.title: libraries
docs.text:
libraries() -> Generator[LibraryFolder, None, None]

Returns the Steam library folders.

Source code in steam_client/library.py 
def libraries(self) -> Generator[LibraryFolder, None, None]:
    """Returns the Steam library folders."""
    if self._is_updated():
        self._libraries_hash = self._hash_steam_libraries()
        self._update_libraries()
    for library in self._libraries:
        yield library

docs.title: games
docs.text:
games() -> Generator[Game, None, None]

Returns the games from the Steam library.

Source code in steam_client/library.py 
def games(self) -> Generator[Game, None, None]:
    """Returns the games from the Steam library."""
    for library in self.libraries():
        for game in library.get_games():
            yield game

docs.title: game_by_id
docs.text:
game_by_id(appid: str) -> Optional[Game]

Returns the game with the specified ID.

Source code in steam_client/library.py 
def game_by_id(self, appid: str) -> Optional[Game]:
    """Returns the game with the specified ID."""
    for game in self.games():
        if game.appid == appid:
            return game
    return None

docs.title: game_by_name
docs.text:
game_by_name(name: str) -> Optional[Game]

Returns the game with the specified name.

Source code in steam_client/library.py 
def game_by_name(self, name: str) -> Optional[Game]:
    """Returns the game with the specified name."""
    for game in self.games():
        if game.name.casefold() == name.casefold():
            return game
    return None

docs.title: shortcuts
docs.text:
shortcuts() -> Generator[Shortcut, None, None]

Returns the Non-Steam shortcuts from the Steam library.

Source code in steam_client/library.py 
def shortcuts(self) -> Generator[Shortcut, None, None]:
    """Returns the Non-Steam shortcuts from the Steam library."""
    for user in self._steam.users:
        for shortcut in user.shortcuts():
            yield shortcut

docs.title: all
docs.text:
all() -> Generator[Union[Game, Shortcut], None, None]

Returns all the games and shortcuts from the Steam library.

Source code in steam_client/library.py 
def all(self) -> Generator[Union[Game, Shortcut], None, None]:
    """Returns all the games and shortcuts from the Steam library."""
    for game in self.games():
        yield game
    for shortcut in self.shortcuts():
        yield shortcut

docs.title: Steam
docs.text:
The Steam class is the main entry point for the library. Instantiate it with a path to a local Steam installation, or use steam_from_registry() on Windows to auto-detect the path.

from steam_client import Steam

steam = Steam()

docs.title: Users
docs.text:
Provides access to previously logged-in Steam accounts read from loginusers.vdf.

for user in steam.users:
    print(user.user.data.PersonaName, user.is_most_recent)

docs.title: shortcuts
docs.text:
shortcuts() -> List[Shortcut]

Returns the data from the shortcuts.vdf file.

Source code in steam_client/login_users.py 
def shortcuts(self) -> List[Shortcut]:
    """Returns the data from the shortcuts.vdf file."""
    with open(self.shortcuts_file, 'rb') as f:
        shortcuts = vdf.binary_load(f)
    return [Shortcut(self, shortcuts['shortcuts'][shortcut_idx]) for shortcut_idx in shortcuts['shortcuts']]

docs.title: LoginUsers
docs.text:
LoginUsers(base_path: str)

Represents the loginusers.vdf file.

Source code in steam_client/login_users.py 
def __init__(self, base_path: str):
    self._base_path = Path(base_path)

docs.title: users
docs.text:
users() -> List[LoginUser]

Returns the users from the loginusers.vdf file.

Source code in steam_client/login_users.py 
def users(self) -> List[LoginUser]:
    """Returns the users from the loginusers.vdf file."""
    with open(self._path, 'r', encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore') as f:
        login_users = vdf.load(f)
    return [
        LoginUser(str(self._base_path), User(int(user_id), user_data))
        for user_id, user_data in login_users['users'].items()
    ]

docs.title: most_recent_user
docs.text:
most_recent_user() -> Optional[LoginUser]

Returns the most recent user from the loginusers.vdf file.

Source code in steam_client/login_users.py 
def most_recent_user(self) -> Optional[LoginUser]:
    """Returns the most recent user from the loginusers.vdf file."""
    return next((user for user in self.users() if user.is_most_recent), None)

docs.title: Utilities
docs.text:
Helper functions for platform-specific setup.

Windows only

steam_from_registry() and get_steam_from_registry() read from the Windows registry and raise OSError on other platforms.

from steam_client.utils import steam_from_registry

steam = steam_from_registry()

docs.title: get_steam_from_registry
docs.text:
get_steam_from_registry() -> str

Returns the Steam install path from the Windows registry.

Source code in steam_client/utils.py 
def get_steam_from_registry() -> str:
    """Returns the Steam install path from the Windows registry."""
    if sys.platform != "win32":
        raise OSError("Windows registry is only available on Windows")

    reg = importlib.import_module("winreg")
    with reg.OpenKey(reg.HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE, STEAM_SUB_KEY) as hkey:
        return reg.QueryValueEx(hkey, "InstallPath")[0]

docs.title: steam_from_registry
docs.text:
steam_from_registry() -> Steam

Returns a Steam instance from the Windows registry.

Source code in steam_client/utils.py 
def steam_from_registry() -> Steam:
    """Returns a Steam instance from the Windows registry."""
    return Steam(get_steam_from_registry())
